ranking.title: BM25 beats raw search ranking
ranking.subtitle: Search engines sort by ads & SEO. We sort by relevance.
ranking.whyDesc: BM25 scores content density, authority domains get +2.0, fresh pages +1.5, code-heavy pages +1.0. Search engines can't do this without access to page content.

free.desc: No OpenAI, Google API, Bing API. Only direct scraping.

tools.title: 8 Tools
tools.desc: From quick answers to deep research.

codeShowcase.title: Perplexity-level answers, zero API cost
codeShowcase.subtitle: One line of Python. No OpenAI bill.
codeShowcase.code1:
from maru_deep_pro_search.tools import answer

result = answer('What is quantum error correction?')
print(result)
codeShowcase.output1:
Quantum error correction (QEC) protects quantum information from decoherence and noise using redundancy across multiple physical qubits [1]. The surface code is the most promising scheme, tolerating error rates up to ~1% [2].

Sources:
[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_error_correction
[2] https://arxiv.org/abs/1208.0928
codeShowcase.code2:
from maru_deep_pro_search.research.deep import deep_research

report = deep_research('Solid state battery 2024')
print(report)
codeShowcase.output2:
# Solid-State Battery Technology in 2024

Solid-state batteries reached a critical inflection point in 2024. Toyota targets 2027 for mass production of sulfide-based cells [1], while Samsung SDI began trial production [2].

Key milestones:
- Energy density: 500+ Wh/kg demonstrated [3]
- Cycle life: >1000 cycles at 80% retention [4]
- Dry electrode coating reduces costs 30% [5]

Sources:
[1] https://global.toyota/...
[2] https://news.samsung.com/...
[3] https://www.nature.com/...
[4] https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/...
[5] https://www.sciencedirect.com/...
codeShowcase.code3:
from maru_deep_pro_search.tools import search_with_citations

sources = search_with_citations('LLM context window scaling')
for s in sources:
    print('[%s] %s — %s' % (s.id, s.title, s.url))
codeShowcase.output3:
[1] Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overtraining — https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.12345
[2] Context Window Extension via Position Interpolation — https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.15595
[3] LongLoRA: Efficient Fine-tuning of Long-Context LLMs — https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.12307
[4] Extending Context Window of Large Language Models — https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.15909
[5] LM-Infinite: Zero-Shot Extreme Length Generalization — https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.16137
